chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Who were weavers?
questions.answer: Weavers were people who made cloth by weaving yarn on looms.

questions.question: What were Indian textiles famous for?
questions.answer: Indian textiles were famous for their fine quality, beautiful designs and durability.

questions.question: Name one famous type of Indian cloth exported to other countries.
questions.answer: Chintz was one famous type of Indian cloth exported to other countries.

questions.question: What is a loom?
questions.answer: A loom is a machine or tool used to weave yarn into cloth.

questions.question: Who were the gomasthas?
questions.answer: Gomasthas were agents of the East India Company who supervised weavers.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company appoint gomasthas?
questions.answer: The Company appointed gomasthas to control weavers and ensure a steady supply of cloth.

questions.question: What was the main raw material used by weavers?
questions.answer: Cotton was the main raw material used by weavers.

questions.question: What was the effect of British rule on Indian weavers?
questions.answer: Many Indian weavers faced difficulties and loss of livelihood due to British policies.

questions.question: What is spinning?
questions.answer: Spinning is the process of making yarn from cotton fibres.

questions.question: What is weaving?
questions.answer: Weaving is the process of interlacing yarn to produce cloth.

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles in great demand in Europe?
questions.answer: They were popular because of their fine quality, bright colours and unique patterns.

questions.question: What was the role of merchants in the textile trade?
questions.answer: Merchants bought cloth from weavers and sold it in markets or exported it.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company want control over textile trade?
questions.answer: The Company wanted control to earn profits from the textile trade.

questions.question: What problem did weavers face under Company rule?
questions.answer: Weavers were forced to sell their cloth only to the Company at low prices.

questions.question: What were advances given by the Company to weavers?
questions.answer: Advances were loans given to weavers to produce cloth for the Company.

questions.question: Why did many weavers accept advances from the Company?
questions.answer: They accepted advances because they needed money to buy raw materials.

questions.question: What is a factory?
questions.answer: A factory is a place where goods are produced using machines and workers.

questions.question: Why did machine-made cloth become popular?
questions.answer: Machine-made cloth was cheaper and produced in large quantities.

questions.question: What effect did machine-made cloth have on Indian weavers?
questions.answer: It reduced demand for handwoven cloth and caused difficulties for weavers.

questions.question: Who were factory owners?
questions.answer: Factory owners were people who invested money to establish and run factories.

questions.question: What role did factories play in textile production?
questions.answer: Factories produced cloth using machines in large quantities.

questions.question: Why did textile mills start in India?
questions.answer: Textile mills started to compete with imported British cloth.

questions.question: Name one city where textile mills were established.
questions.answer: Bombay was one of the cities where textile mills were established.

questions.question: What was one major change brought by industrialization?
questions.answer: Industrialization increased machine production and factory work.

questions.question: How did industrialization affect traditional industries?
questions.answer: Industrialization reduced the importance of traditional handloom industries.
